items.title: Morikawa holds off epic Scheffler comeback to edge Pebble Beach thriller
items.summary: Collin Morikawa claimed a first PGA Tour title since 2023 with a dramatic one-shot victory at the AT&T Pebble Beach Pro-Am, where Scottie Scheffler threatened a historic comeback.

items.title: Samenvatting: Ajax heeft aan goed half uur genoeg tegen Fortuna Sittard
items.summary: Ajax heeft zaterdagavond een probleemloze overwinning geboekt op Fortuna Sittard . De Amsterdammers namen binnen een halfuur al ruim afstand van Fortuna Sittard, dat uiteindelijk vier Belgische treffers om de oren kreeg.

items.title: Samenvatting: Feyenoord komt in blessuretijd goed weg tegen Go Ahead Eagles
items.summary: Feyenoord heeft zondag ternauwernood pijnlijk puntenverlies voorkomen tegen Go Ahead Eagles . Met een man meer stond het héél lang 0-0, maar Casper Tengstedt redde het duel voor de Rotterdammers met een rake strafschop in blessuretijd.

items.title: Samenvatting: PSV verliest in Volendam weer eens een uitduel
items.summary: PSV heeft voor het eerst sinds augustus 2025 weer eens een Eredivisie-duel verloren . In het KRAS Stadion kwamen de Eindhovenaren vrijdag zwak voor de dag tegen promovendus FC Volendam. De recordbrekende uitreeks van PSV blijft zodoende steken op zestien overwinningen op rij.

items.title: 'Met Malen vanaf het begin hadden we meer punten gehad'
items.summary: Donyell Malen kent een fantastische start bij AS Roma. Trainer Gian Piero Gasperini denkt dat zijn elftal hoger had gestaan als hij vanaf de start van het seizoen de beschikking had gehad over de winteraanwinst.

items.title: Sporting Portugal houdt titelstrijd met Farioli's Porto nog open
items.summary: Zondag stonden er vier wedstrijden in de Eredivisie op het programma. Het duel van Feyenoord volgden we in een aparte live. In deze VI Live bleef je op de hoogte van SC Heerenveen tegen PEC Zwolle, Telstar tegen FC Twente, Sparta Rotterdam tegen NEC én het belangrijkste buitenlandse voetbal.

items.title: Purpose and togetherness: What the soccer world can learn from FA Cup heroes Macclesfield
items.summary: Macclesfield produced one of soccer's greatest Cinderella stories in beating Crystal Palace in the FA Cup, but the tale behind the triumph is even more remarkable.

items.title: European football: Serie A referees’ chief apologises after controversial Kalulu red card
items.summary: Juventus lost 3-2 in dramatic fashion away at Inter Santos scores on debut to earn Napoli draw with Roma Serie A’s referee designator Gianluca Rocchi said the match official Federico La Penna was “clearly wrong” in showing the Juventus defender Pierre Kalulu a second yellow card during Saturday’s loss at Inter, and apologised over the incident. Kalulu was sent off after Inter’s Alessandro Bastoni tumbled to the ground and immediately gestured towards the referee demanding a card, indicating that Kalulu had grabbed his shirt to bring him down. Television footage suggested there was no contact between the players. Juventus, down to 10 men after the sending off, lost 3-2, meaning Inter are now eight points clear at the top. Continue reading...

items.title: James Trafford could leave Manchester City after being frozen out by Donnarumma
items.summary: Keeper did not think Guardiola would sign Italian ‘It’s football, you’ve got to keep grafting every day’ James Trafford has admitted he did not expect Manchester City to sign Gianluigi Donnarumma after his transfer from Burnley last summer, with the deputy goalkeeper potentially leaving this summer. Trafford returned to City after two years at Turf Moor on 31 July in a deal worth £31m before Pep Guardiola informed the club executive that the manager also wanted Donnarumma. The Italian joined on 2 September from Paris Saint-Germain for £26m and became Guardiola’s first choice. Continue reading...

items.summary: Four teams in the title race and three points separating the top three - this was a weekend at the top of the Scottish Premiership that swung the race towards a 'race to the wire' contest.

items.summary: Mikel Arteta admitted a mounting injury list could leave Arsenal vulnerable in their pursuit of silverware this season.

items.title: No notes: FA Cup GK is hero despite research fail
items.summary: Leeds United's Lucas Perri was the penalty shootout hero despite his preparation being foiled by Birmingham City.

items.title: FA Cup 'mud bath' survived by PL strugglers
items.summary: Wolves boss Rob Edwards described his side's 1-0 FA Cup win at Grimsby as an "aggressive game of headers and volleys" as the Premier League club survived a mud bath.

items.title: Macclesfield's Rooney: 'Ambition' to manage higher
items.summary: John Rooney is clear in his ambition to manage at the highest level possible but he wants to go on that journey with Macclesfield.

items.title: De Minaur reigns in Rotterdam | Shelton beats Fritz to Dallas title
items.summary: Alex de Minaur claimed his 11th ATP title after beating Felix Auger-Aliassime in straight sets in the final of the Rotterdam Open.

items.title: The big VAR debate: with, without or change its scope?
items.summary: After a weekend of FA Cup action without VAR, BBC Sport ask managers, fans and pundits whether the game needs technology - and what needs to change.

items.title: 183 team changes in 14 ties - but does it really matter for the FA Cup?
items.summary: Does 183 team changes in 14 ties devalue the FA Cup? Chief football writer Phil McNulty insists the cup retains its old magic.

items.title: Celtic spirit reminds O'Neill of glory years
items.summary: Interim manager Martin O'Neill says the current side's fighting spirit reminds him of his previous Celtic glory years after their comeback win in Kilmarnock.
